description_long: This is a weathered stone grave marker, likely a headstone, set into a rectangular patch of grass. The headstone features a rounded top with a carved circular motif and a diamond-shaped cutout at its center, reminiscent of a Celtic cross design. The stone surface is aged, with visible discoloration, moss, and erosion, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for many years. The base of the headstone is embedded in a grassy mound, with the lower portion showing signs of soil and organic growth. No visible inscriptions or engravings can be discerned from the images, and the overall structure appears solid and monolithic. The object is typical of traditional grave markers found in older cemeteries or churchyards.
description: A weathered stone headstone with a rounded top, diamond cutout, and mossy base set in a grassy patch.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the headstone and the grassy base.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the rounded top, central diamond cutout, and weathered stone surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness and mossy base of the headstone.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the headstone, rounded top, and grassy mound at the base.
